phase.name: QLoRA Infrastructure
phase.description: Add bitsandbytes as optional dependency, extend ModelFamily enum, add availability check, update VRAM dispatch to use mode string.

task.title: Add bitsandbytes optional dep, ModelFamily.SAM3_QLORA, cleanup SAM3_LORA
task.description:
1. Add bitsandbytes to pyproject.toml as OPTIONAL dependency: [project.optional-dependencies] qlora = ["bitsandbytes>=0.45"] (NOT core — bnb requires CUDA, breaks macOS/CPU installs)
2. Add SAM3_QLORA = "sam3_qlora" to ModelFamily enum.
3. Remove stale SAM3_LORA = "sam3_lora" from ModelFamily enum (deprecated, no adapter implementation, no build_adapter case).
4. Add _bnb_available() utility to model_builder.py: def _bnb_available() -> bool: try: import bitsandbytes; return True except ImportError: return False
5. Add _BNB_INSTALL_INSTRUCTIONS with clear error message: "SAM3 QLoRA requires bitsandbytes. Install: uv add bitsandbytes"
Note: peft is already a core dependency (pyproject.toml line 35). Do NOT add it again.

tests.test: test ModelFamily.SAM3_QLORA enum value exists and equals "sam3_qlora"
test ModelFamily has no SAM3_LORA value (removed)
test ModelConfig accepts architecture_params with quantize_4bit=True
test _bnb_available() returns bool without crashing

task.title: Refactor VRAM check: encoder_frozen → vram_mode string dispatch
task.description:
REVIEWER FINDING: The current _require_sam3(encoder_frozen: bool) creates a mode gap for QLoRA (encoder not frozen, but VRAM is 6 GB not 16 GB).
Refactor: 1. Change _require_sam3() signature from encoder_frozen: bool to vram_mode: str accepting "inference", "training", "qlora".
2. Add MIN_VRAM_QLORA_MB = 6_144 (6 GB — same as inference, because QLoRA saves weight memory but activation memory dominates on ViTs). Original plan said 4 GB but arXiv:2502.00418 shows this is wrong.
3. Update build_adapter() dispatch table: SAM3_VANILLA → vram_mode="inference" (6 GB) SAM3_TOPOLORA → vram_mode="training" (16 GB) SAM3_HYBRID → vram_mode="inference" (6 GB) SAM3_QLORA → vram_mode="qlora" (6 GB)
4. Add _require_bnb() check before QLoRA — raises RuntimeError with install instructions if bitsandbytes not available.

tests.test: test check_sam3_vram(mode="qlora") passes with 6 GB+ VRAM (mocked)
test check_sam3_vram(mode="qlora") raises RuntimeError with 4 GB (mocked)
test check_sam3_vram rejects unknown mode strings
test _require_sam3 accepts vram_mode string parameter
test build_adapter(SAM3_QLORA) raises RuntimeError when bnb not installed (mock _bnb_available() → False)

task.title: Sam3QLoraBackbone — quantized backbone with peft LoRA
task.description:
Create sam3_qlora_backbone.py wrapping Sam3Model with BitsAndBytesConfig for 4-bit NF4 quantization + peft LoRA adapters.
REVIEWER FINDINGS incorporated: 1. target_modules: ["q_proj", "v_proj"] (confirmed from Sam3ViTRoPEAttention) Configurable via architecture_params.lora_targets for experimentation.
2. Gradient checkpointing is MANDATORY (not optional) because ViTs are activation-bound. Call model.gradient_checkpointing_enable( gradient_checkpointing_kwargs={"use_reentrant": False}).
3. "Late QLoRA" mode: when architecture_params.lora_layers is set (e.g., [24,25,26,27,28,29,30,31]), apply LoRA only to those layers. This provides genuine VRAM savings on ViTs.
4. Lazy import of bitsandbytes — ImportError produces clear RuntimeError with installation instructions (never silent-fail).
5. Shares preprocessing logic with Sam3Backbone (_preprocess method). Extract to a shared mixin or utility if duplication exceeds 3 methods.
Loading flow: bnb_config = BitsAndBytesConfig( load_in_4bit=True, bnb_4bit_quant_type="nf4", bnb_4bit_compute_dtype=torch.float16, # AMP handles this bnb_4bit_use_double_quant=True, ) model = Sam3Model.from_pretrained("facebook/sam3", quantization_config=bnb_config, device_map="auto", token=hf_token) model.gradient_checkpointing_enable( gradient_checkpointing_kwargs={"use_reentrant": False}) peft_config = LoraConfig(r=16, lora_alpha=32, target_modules=["q_proj", "v_proj"], ...) model = get_peft_model(model, peft_config) self.encoder = model.vision_encoder

tests.test: test importing sam3_qlora_backbone without bnb raises RuntimeError with install instructions (not ImportError)
test Sam3QLoraBackbone creates without error (requires SAM3+GPU+bnb)
test quantized model weights are frozen (requires_grad=False)
test LoRA adapter weights are trainable (requires_grad=True)
test gradient checkpointing is enabled on encoder
test extract_features returns correct shape (B, 256, H_feat, W_feat)
test total trainable params <5M (LoRA on q_proj+v_proj only)
test late_qlora mode (lora_layers=[24..31]) applies LoRA to 8 layers only

phase.name: QLoRA Adapter
phase.description: Build Sam3QLoraAdapter implementing ModelAdapter ABC. Handle checkpoint save/load for quantized+peft models. Handle ONNX export via dequantize-merge-export pipeline. Wire into model_builder factory.

task.title: Sam3QLoraAdapter — full adapter with checkpoint and ONNX handling
task.description:
Create sam3_qlora.py implementing Sam3QLoraAdapter(ModelAdapter).
REVIEWER FINDINGS incorporated:
1. CHECKPOINT FORMAT (CRITICAL — peft save_pretrained creates directory): save_checkpoint(path) creates a directory at path.with_suffix(''): path_dir/ decoder_state_dict.pt — standard torch.save peft_adapter/ — peft save_pretrained directory adapter_config.json adapter_model.safetensors manifest.json — links both, records peft version load_checkpoint(path) must: a. Re-create quantized backbone from scratch (BitsAndBytesConfig) b. Load peft adapter via model.load_adapter() c. Load decoder state_dict
2. ONNX EXPORT (BLOCKER — bnb NF4 has no ONNX operator): export_onnx() implements dequantize-merge-export pipeline: a. Load base Sam3Model in FP16 (not quantized) — ~1.7 GB b. Load peft adapter onto FP16 model c. merge_and_unload() — merges LoRA into base weights d. Export merged FP16 model to ONNX via _LogitsWrapper pattern Note: merge_and_unload() FAILS on quantized models but works on FP16. This means ONNX export needs ~1.7 GB + activations. Add warning.
3. DECODER REUSE (IMPORTANT — avoid double model load): Do NOT instantiate a separate Sam3MaskDecoder (which loads full model). Instead, extract decoder from the already-loaded quantized model: self.decoder_module = model.sam_mask_decoder # from same load If Sam3MaskDecoder must be used, add constructor param to accept a pre-loaded decoder module.
4. merge_and_unload() is NOT available on quantized models. Never call it except during ONNX export (which uses a separate FP16 model load).

tests.test: test adapter creates from ModelConfig with SAM3_QLORA family
test forward produces SegmentationOutput with 2-class logits
test forward output shape matches input spatial dims (B, 2, D, H, W)
test trainable_parameters returns count (LoRA + decoder only)
test gradient flows through LoRA adapters (backward produces nonzero grads)
test get_config returns variant="qlora" and lora_rank info
test save_checkpoint creates directory with manifest.json + peft_adapter/
test save_checkpoint + load_checkpoint round-trip preserves weights
test export_onnx uses dequantize-merge pipeline (produces valid ONNX)
test adapter raises RuntimeError on CPU-only system (not opaque CUDA error)

task.title: Wire SAM3_QLORA into model_builder factory
task.description: Add SAM3_QLORA case to build_adapter() in model_builder.py. Dispatch chain: 1. Check _sam3_package_available() — same as other SAM3 variants 2. Check _bnb_available() — QLoRA-specific, raises with install instructions 3. Check VRAM via _require_sam3(config, vram_mode="qlora") 4. Check HF token via require_hf_token() 5. Return Sam3QLoraAdapter(config)

tests.test: test build_adapter(SAM3_QLORA config) returns Sam3QLoraAdapter
test build_adapter(SAM3_QLORA) raises RuntimeError when SAM3 missing
test build_adapter(SAM3_QLORA) raises RuntimeError when bnb missing
test error message includes "uv add bitsandbytes" install instructions

phase.name: Bug Fixes and Config Cleanup
phase.description: Fix V3 Hybrid gradient bug. Clean up misleading config values. Update no-stub tests for new variant.

task.title: Fix V3 Hybrid gradient bug — .detach() + refactor inline fusion
task.description:
REVIEWER FINDINGS (architecture + TDD) — expanded scope:
BUG: axial_proj (Conv3d, ~197K params) never receives gradients.
ROOT CAUSE: Two .detach() sites plus code duplication.
1. Sam3HybridAdapter.forward() line 213: sam_projected = self.fusion.proj(sam_features.detach()) → .detach() cuts gradient to axial_proj (which sits between frozen encoder and this call)
2. GatedFeatureFusion.forward() line 80: projected: Tensor = self.proj(f_sam.detach()) → Same bug, but this method is DEAD CODE (never called by adapter — adapter manually reimplements the fusion logic)
3. The adapter's forward() bypasses self.fusion() entirely, calling self.fusion.proj() and self.fusion.gate_alpha directly (lines 213-223). This code duplication is what caused the bug.
FIX (3 parts): a. Remove .detach() from line 213 of Sam3HybridAdapter.forward() (torch.no_grad() on SAM encoder already prevents gradient to frozen params) b. Remove .detach() from line 80 of GatedFeatureFusion.forward() (prevent same bug if someone refactors to use self.fusion() properly) c. Refactor Sam3HybridAdapter.forward() to actually call self.fusion(logits, sam_features) instead of reimplementing inline

tests.test: test axial_proj weights receive gradients after backward pass
test GatedFeatureFusion.proj weights receive gradients
test GatedFeatureFusion.gate_alpha receives gradients
test frozen SAM3 encoder params still have requires_grad=False
test forward output shape unchanged after fix
test self.fusion() is called (not bypassed) in forward()

task.title: Clean up SAM3 YAML configs + create QLoRA configs
task.description:
1. Remove "pretrained: false" from all SAM3 model/experiment YAMLs (SAM3 ALWAYS loads pretrained — field is ignored but misleading) 2. Change V1 vanilla baseline loss to cbdice_cldice (project default) 3. Create configs/model/sam3_qlora.yaml with: - quantize_4bit: true - lora_rank: 16 - lora_alpha: 32 - lora_targets: [q_proj, v_proj] - gradient_checkpointing: true 4. Create configs/experiment/sam3_qlora_topology.yaml 5. Create configs/experiment/sam3_late_qlora_topology.yaml (lora_layers: [24,25,26,27,28,29,30,31]) 6. Create configs/experiment/sam3_comparison.yaml (all variants)
Note: T-07 depends on T-01 because configs reference sam3_qlora model family.

tests.test: test all SAM3 model YAML configs parse without errors
test no SAM3 config contains "pretrained: false"
test sam3_qlora.yaml specifies quantize_4bit: true
test sam3_late_qlora has lora_layers with 8 entries
test sam3_comparison.yaml lists all 5 variants (V1-V4b)

task.title: Update enforcement tests for SAM3_QLORA
task.description: 1. Add sam3_qlora.py and sam3_qlora_backbone.py to no-stub AST checks. 2. Update VRAM check tests for new "qlora" mode. 3. Add _bnb_available() to the import guard test matrix.

tests.test: test sam3_qlora.py has no stub/fallback patterns (AST check)
test sam3_qlora_backbone.py has no stub/fallback patterns (AST check)
test VRAM check mode="qlora" is covered

phase.name: Integration and Observability
phase.description: Feature caching for V1/V3, VRAM profiling for empirical verification, documentation update.

task.title: Feature caching Prefect task for V1/V3
task.description:
Add a Prefect task in the training flow that optionally runs feature extraction before training when model_family is sam3_vanilla or sam3_hybrid and architecture_params.use_cached_features is true.
Flow: extract features → save to cache_dir → train decoder on cached features.
The existing sam3_feature_cache.py module handles extraction and loading. This task wires it into the Prefect training flow.
IMPORTANT: cache_dir must be a Docker volume mount (/data/sam3_cache), NOT /tmp (per CLAUDE.md rule #18).

tests.test: test training flow config accepts use_cached_features param
test cache_dir is not under /tmp (Docker volume compliance)
test training with cached features loads from cache_dir

task.title: VRAM profiling utility for empirical verification
task.description:
Add a lightweight VRAM profiler that logs peak GPU memory at key points. Uses torch.cuda.reset_peak_memory_stats() before each phase, then torch.cuda.max_memory_allocated() after.
Measurement methodology (from TDD reviewer): 1. torch.cuda.reset_peak_memory_stats() 2. Execute phase (model load / forward / backward) 3. peak_mb = torch.cuda.max_memory_allocated() / 1e6 4. Log to MLflow artifacts as JSON
Output: JSON dict with keys: model_load_mb, encoder_forward_peak_mb, decoder_forward_peak_mb, backward_peak_mb, optimizer_step_peak_mb.
Works on CPU too (returns zeros, no crash).

tests.test: test VRAMProfiler records peak memory at each stage (mocked)
test VRAMProfiler.report() returns dict with expected keys
test VRAMProfiler works on CPU (returns zeros, no crash)
test VRAMProfiler.reset() clears peak stats

task.title: Update adapter CLAUDE.md with V4 QLoRA info
task.description: Update src/minivess/adapters/CLAUDE.md: - Add V4 QLoRA and V4b Late QLoRA to VRAM table - Add sam3_qlora.py and sam3_qlora_backbone.py to Key Files table - Add note about ViTs being activation-bound (QLoRA != LLM QLoRA) - Remove any remaining "pretrained: false" references - Note ONNX export requires dequantize-merge pipeline for QLoRA

tests.test: test CLAUDE.md contains "qlora" (case-insensitive grep)

gate.criterion: Sam3QLoraBackbone loads 4-bit quantized SAM3 model. Measurement: torch.cuda.reset_peak_memory_stats() before load, torch.cuda.max_memory_allocated() after load shows <1 GB for model weights (vs 1.68 GB FP16). Confirms quantization works.
gate.action_if_fail: Check bitsandbytes version compatibility with CUDA 12.8. Verify bnb_4bit_compute_dtype matches GPU capabilities. Try load_in_8bit as fallback. If still fails, drop QLoRA variant.

gate.criterion: Sam3QLoraAdapter forward+backward with peak VRAM <8 GB (measured, not estimated). Input: 1×1×3×64×64 (internally processed at 1008×1008 by SAM3 — the 64×64 input does NOT reduce internal resolution). Methodology: reset_peak_memory_stats before forward, measure after backward + optimizer.step().
gate.action_if_fail: Step 1: Switch to late QLoRA (layers 24-31 only). Step 2: Reduce LoRA rank from 16 to 8. Step 3: Reduce to 2 depth slices instead of 3. Step 4: If still exceeds 8 GB, QLoRA is cloud-only like V2 TopoLoRA.

gate.criterion: V3 Hybrid: axial_proj.weight.grad.abs().sum() > 0 AND fusion.proj.weight.grad.abs().sum() > 0 AND fusion.gate_alpha.grad is not None — all after a single backward pass. Expressible as a standard pytest assertion.
gate.action_if_fail: Trace gradient flow with register_hook() on intermediate tensors to find where it breaks. The .detach() removal should be sufficient given torch.no_grad() on the SAM encoder.

training_plan.description:
After all tasks are DONE, run the SAM3 comparison study. Same dataset (MiniVess 70 vols), same loss (cbdice_cldice), same epochs (50) — only model_family changes.
Academic story: 5 SAM3 adaptation strategies compared on the same biomedical segmentation task, scaling from 8 GB desktop to A100 cloud.

run.estimated_vram: ~6-8 GB (borderline — may need late QLoRA fallback)

run.note: Fallback if R2 OOMs. Also interesting comparison: full vs late LoRA.
Cloud-only. Use SkyPilot: sky launch deployment/skypilot/train_generic.yaml -c sam3-topolora --env MODEL_FAMILY=sam3_topolora

comparison.hypothesis: V1 (vanilla frozen) < V4 (QLoRA) ≈ V2 (TopoLoRA) < DynUNet baseline. V3 (hybrid) may exceed DynUNet if gated fusion learns useful features. Key academic findings: 1. QLoRA vs standard LoRA quality gap on biomedical ViT segmentation 2. Full QLoRA vs late QLoRA (layers 24-31): VRAM vs quality tradeoff 3. Whether quantization-induced noise hurts fine-grained vessel topology 4. Demonstration that ViTs are activation-bound (QLoRA ≠ LLM experience)

reviewer.name: Technical Feasibility
reviewer.finding: QLoRA on ViTs provides negligible VRAM savings vs LoRA (~0.1-1.5 GB). ViTs are activation-bound. VRAM estimate revised from 3-4 to 6-8 GB. Source: arXiv:2502.00418 (Tischer et al., 2025)
peft target_modules: q_proj, v_proj, o_proj, fc1, fc2 in Sam3ViTRoPEAttention. Default: ["q_proj", "v_proj"]. Added to T-03 description.
Gradient checkpointing needs use_reentrant=False on PyTorch 2.5+. Added to T-03 loading flow.
VRAM estimate 3-4 GB was wrong. Revised to 6-8 GB. Gate G1 updated.
peft save/load works for 4-bit. Gotcha: merge_and_unload fails on quantized models. Added to T-04 description.
ONNX export impossible from bnb NF4. Dequantize-merge-export pipeline added to T-04. Requires ~1.7 GB for separate FP16 model load.

reviewer.name: Architecture Coherence
reviewer.finding: Custom LoRA (V2) vs peft LoRA (V4) comparison fairness. Decision: Keep both — they are intentionally different approaches. V2 = custom LoRA on all layers. V4 = peft LoRA on targeted modules + 4-bit. Document as intentional comparison variable in the paper.
Missing shared backbone abstraction (Sam3BackboneBase mixin). Deferred — extract only if duplication exceeds 3 methods.
Double SAM3 model load (backbone + decoder independently). Added to T-04: extract decoder from already-loaded model.
peft save_pretrained creates directory, not .pt file. Checkpoint format redesigned in T-04 description.
VRAM mode dispatch gap. Changed to vram_mode string in T-02.
V3 bug fix expanded: remove both .detach() sites + refactor inline fusion back to self.fusion() call. Updated T-06.
T-07 missing dependency on T-01. Fixed.
Test coverage gaps (ONNX, CPU, integration). Added to T-04/T-05.
Stale SAM3_LORA enum. Added removal to T-01.
_LogitsWrapper duplication. ONNX export may change for QLoRA anyway.
pretrained: false field confusion. Cleanup in T-07.
bitsandbytes should be optional dep. Changed to [qlora] extra in T-01.

reviewer.name: TDD Compliance
reviewer.finding: No CI skip-marker strategy. Added _qlora_skip marker definition and Tier 1/Tier 2 test split to plan header.
No CPU-only test isolation. Each task now has tier="1" or tier="2" annotations on tests. Tier 1 always runs in CI.
RED phase on CI produces zero failures. Added note that RED verification for GPU tests must run on development machine.
Gate measurement methodology. Added reset_peak_memory_stats pattern to G0 and G1 criteria.
bitsandbytes not installed → uncontrolled ImportError. Added _bnb_available() + _BNB_INSTALL_INSTRUCTIONS to T-01. Added graceful-failure test to T-05.
peft available but bnb not → opaque crash. Added explicit check and RuntimeError in T-03 description.
4 GB VRAM threshold too tight. Raised to 6 GB in T-02.
No test file paths. Added test_file elements to all tasks.
T-11 had "no tests". Added grep-based documentation test.
TODO → NOT_STARTED vocabulary alignment.
T-06 bug description conflated two .detach() call sites. Clarified.
peft already in pyproject.toml. Noted in T-01.
